# Kudbee Quant

> Kudbee Quant is an honest quantitative research toolkit for crypto and prediction
> markets. It takes the discretionary Traders Reality PVSRA method (vector candles and
> market-maker-cycle context), quantifies it as testable features, and validates it with
> an event-driven backtester, walk-forward analysis, and Monte Carlo — reporting risk as
> loudly as return. It is the deliberate inverse of "AI trading terminal" screenshots:
> no hardcoded performance numbers, every figure computed from real data, every signal a
> hypothesis. Not financial advice; markets do not offer high return at low risk.

## Key facts

- Name: Kudbee Quant
- Category: Honest quantitative trading research toolkit
- Method: Tino's Traders Reality "Hybrid System" — PVSRA + Beat-the-Market-Maker (BTMM)
- Markets: Crypto (Binance) and prediction markets (Polymarket); equities/FX via Yahoo for uncorrelated validation
- Default mode: Paper-trading; live capital is a guarded opt-in with hard drawdown limits
- Differentiator: radical honesty about uncertainty — risk reported as loudly as return
- Status: Private beta with an open early-access waitlist (free to join)
- Not financial advice; trading involves substantial risk of loss

## What it does

- Signal: PVSRA vector candles (Pine Script ported to Python), treated as hypotheses
- Context: market-maker layer — sessions (Asian/London/NY), Asian range, PDH/PDL/PWH/PWL,
  liquidity sweeps, weekly MM cycle, monthly open (M0 pivot)
- Backtest: event-driven, fees + slippage, no lookahead
- Risk engine: Sharpe, Sortino, max drawdown, VaR/CVaR, Calmar, risk-of-ruin, fractional Kelly
- Validation: walk-forward out-of-sample, Monte Carlo, and correlation-adjusted multi-asset

## How it works (Scan, Detect, Validate, Size)

1. Scan: ingest OHLCV from Binance and Yahoo, plus Polymarket prediction-market prices
2. Detect: quantify PVSRA vector candles and market-maker context as testable features
3. Validate: backtest with fees/slippage/no-lookahead, then walk-forward and Monte Carlo
4. Size: only if an edge survives — capped fractional Kelly, hard drawdown limits, paper-first

## The honesty contract (enforced in code)

1. No hardcoded performance numbers — if it can't be computed from real data, it doesn't render
2. Paper-trading is the default; live is a guarded opt-in
3. Every signal is a hypothesis to be measured, never a promise
4. Risk is reported as loudly as return
5. Validation is adversarial — walk-forward and Monte Carlo by default
